# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.
# Category: api_gateway_security

_format_version: "3.0"
_info:
  select_tags:
    - production
  tags:
    - production
    - microservices-gateway

upstreams:
  - name: users-service-upstream
    targets:
      - target: users-service-v1.internal.example.com:8080
        weight: 100
      - target: users-service-v2.internal.example.com:8080
        weight: 100
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /health
        http_get_config:
          headers:
            Host: users-service.internal
        healthy:
          interval: 5
          successes: 3
        unhealthy:
          interval: 10
          failures: 3
        timeout: 2
        concurrency: 10
      passive:
        unhealthy:
          failures: 5
          timeouts: 3
          http_statuses: [500, 502, 503, 504]
    tags:
      - users
      - internal

  - name: products-service-upstream
    targets:
      - target: products-service-a.internal.example.com:3000
        weight: 100
      - target: products-service-b.internal.example.com:3000
        weight: 100
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /status
        healthy:
          interval: 5
          successes: 3
        unhealthy:
          interval: 10
          failures: 3
        timeout: 2
      passive:
        unhealthy:
          failures: 5
          timeouts: 3
          http_statuses: [500, 502, 503, 504]
    tags:
      - products
      - internal

services:
  - name: users-api-service
    host: users-service-upstream
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 5
    read_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    connect_timeout: 5000
    tags:
      - users
      - api
    plugins:
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key_names:
            - X-API-KEY
            - apikey
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 100
          hour: 1000
          policy: local
          fault_tolerant: true # Continue processing if rate-limiting backend is down
      - name: cors
        config:
          origins:
            - 'https://app.example.com'
            - 'https://dev.example.com'
            - 'http://localhost:3000'
          methods:
            - GET
            - POST
            - PUT
            - PATCH
            - DELETE
          headers:
            - Authorization
            - Content-Type
            - X-API-KEY
          exposed_headers:
            - X-Request-ID
            - Link
          credentials: true
          max_age: 3600
      - name: http-log
        config:
          http_endpoint: https://log-aggregator.example.com/kong-logs
          method: POST
          headers:
            Content-Type: application/json
            X-Auth-Token: secret-log-token
          custom_fields_by_lua:
            client_ip: "ngx.var.remote_addr"
            request_id: "ngx.var.request_id"
            user_agent: "ngx.var.http_user_agent"
            # Extract API key for logging purposes (careful with sensitive data)
            # api_key: "ngx.var.http_x_api_key or ngx.var.http_apikey"
          timeout: 5000
          keepalive: 60000
          flush_timeout: 5
          queue_size: 1000
      - name: prometheus
        config:
          per_consumer: true

  - name: products-api-service
    host: products-service-upstream
    port: 80
    protocol: http
    retries: 5
    read_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    connect_timeout: 5000
    tags:
      - products
      - api
    plugins:
      - name: jwt
        config:
          claims_to_verify:
            - exp
            - nbf
          verify_signature: true
          # Kong will try to fetch JWKS from .well-known or use configured secrets
          # For a real setup, configure consumers and their JWKs/secrets
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 200
          policy: local
      - name: cors
        config:
          origins:
            - 'https://app.example.com'
            - 'https://dev.example.com'
          methods:
            - GET
            - POST
          headers:
            - Authorization
            - Content-Type
          credentials: true
          max_age: 3600
      - name: request-transformer
        config:
          add:
            headers:
              X-Internal-Gateway: "Kong"
              X-Request-ID: "$request_id"
          remove:
            headers:
              Via: true
              X-Powered-By: true
              # ... other potentially revealing headers
      - name: http-log
        config:
          http_endpoint: https://log-aggregator.example.com/kong-logs
          method: POST
          headers:
            Content-Type: application/json
            X-Auth-Token: secret-log-token
          custom_fields_by_lua:
            client_ip: "ngx.var.remote_addr"
            request_id: "ngx.var.request_id"
          timeout: 5000
          queue_size: 1000

routes:
  - name: users-api-route
    paths:
      - /users
      - /users/v1
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
    service: users-api-service
    strip_path: true # /users/123 -> /123
    preserve_host: false # Kong will use the upstream's host for the backend request
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    tags:
      - users
      - api
      - public-access

  - name: products-api-route
    paths:
      - /products
      - /products/v1
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
    service: products-api-service
    strip_path: true # /products/item-id -> /item-id
    preserve_host: false
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    tags:
      - products
      - api
      - authenticated-access

  - name: public-gateway-status-route
    paths:
      - /gateway-status
    methods:
      - GET
    # This route uses a direct response plugin and doesn't proxy to a backend service.
    # It still needs to be associated with a service for Kong's internal structure.
    # We can point it to any service, or create a dummy one if no real service is intended.
    service: users-api-service # Placeholder service
    strip_path: false
    preserve_host: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    plugins:
      - name: request-termination # Short-circuit and return a fixed response
        config:
          status_code: 200
          body: "API Gateway is Healthy and Operational"
          content_type: "text/plain"
    tags:
      - monitoring
      - public
      - healthcheck

  - name: global-api-catchall-route
    paths:
      - /
    regex_priority: 0 # Ensure this is lower priority than specific routes
    service: users-api-service # Point to any service, or a dedicated error service
    strip_path: false
    preserve_host: true
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    plugins:
      - name: request-termination
        config:
          status_code: 404
          body: "{\"message\":\"Not Found. The requested resource could not be found.\"}"
          content_type: "application/json"
    tags:
      - error-handling
      - catchall
